example_question: Do you think that having many levels of hierarchy is the best type of organisational structure for a large business? Justify your answer.
model_answer: Do you think that having many levels of hierarchy is the best type of organisational structure for a large business? Justify your answer. • Having levels of hierarchy (tall hierarchy) means a long chain of command which can slow down communication as messages have to pass through many levels to reach the intended person which could slow down decision making • Senior managers are more remote from employees which can demotivate employees and lower efficiency/output • Managers have fewer employees to supervise which could fewer opportunities for delegation • Can provide more opportunities for promotion • Having a wider structure would lead to a short chain of command/each manager having to be responsible for more people which could mean managers lose control Justification might include: • No as it could depend on the leadership style of the manager – if they have an autocratic style – a tall structure may be better as this will allow them to have greater control over what all the employee

example_question: Do you think that all businesses will benefit from operating in a country which is experiencing rapid economic growth? Justify your answer.
model_answer: Do you think that all businesses will benefit from operating in a country which is experiencing rapid economic growth? Justify your answer. • Economic growth increases consumer incomes which increases demand for most goods • Rapid economic growth can lead to higher rate of inflation which raises firms’ costs/reduces profit margin • Patterns of consumer demand might change reducing revenue for some businesses • Could lead to shortage of skilled workers so have to offer pay to attract/retain employees leading to higher costs Justification might include: • No - unlikely all businesses will benefit as it depends on what they sell as if they sell basic goods, customers may switch to more expensive items as their incomes increase leading to fewer sales for some businesses and higher sales for others. example_question: Explain two possible problems for MLC of relocating its operations to another country. Problem 1: Problem 2:
model_answer: Explain two possible problems for MLC of relocating its operations to another country. • Access to suitable employees/not easy to find employees as at new site will need 150 new employees increasing recruitment/training costs • Access to/cost of suitable land as will need a big space to make motorcycles • Different legal controls/standards in the new factory which may mean have to change how/where operate • Could be further away from market/suppliers/need to find new suppliers which increases transport costs • Communication problems as may need to learn a new language so takes longer to complete on-the-job training Question Answer
notes: Marks 6

example_question: Do you think on-the-job training is a better method of training for a manufacturing business to use than off-the-job training? Justify your answer.
model_answer: Do you think on-the-job training is a better method of training for a manufacturing business to use than off-the-job training? Justify your answer. Off-the-job training: • Not necessarily tailored to individual business • Have to pay more/high cost for specialists which increases costs • Skills might not be available in the business so benefit from techniques/ideas • Allows for mistakes to be made when learning so less likely to waste resources when working/lower risk of damaging On-the-job training: • Can reinforce errors/bad habits of trainer reducing sales/damage reputation • Fewer new ideas/skills will be taught • Control what is taught/employees learn exact way business wants things done so workers only learn skills needed • Employees can carry on working so some output is still produced • Cheaper (than off-the-job) Justification may include: • No, as it depends what the training is for – if using new techniques/ machines the business may not have a suitable person to train others
notes: new business reputation [an] Marks 2

example_question: Do you think selling to different market segments is the best way for a small business to respond to increased competition? Justify your answer.

Targeting a new segment: • Wider market base could increase sales revenue • Spreads risk as not reliant on one market for sales • Need to know reaction/current level of competition as customers may be loyal to other businesses so not easy to • High cost and time involved developing new products • Cost of promotion as need to attract customer’s attention Other issues or ways could be discussed such as: • Could change prices e.g. lower prices could lead to lower profit margin/seen as lower quality which could lower sales • Size of market could be a factor as if niche market this will limit number of possible sales Justification might include: • Yes, selling to other segments means spreading risk as not reliant on one market for sales . Do you think selecting employees with the right personality is more 6 important than their experience for a tertiary sector business? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant issues. Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award 2 marks for justified decision as to whether selecting employees with the right personality is more important than their experience for a tertiary sector business. Points might include: Personality: • More likely to make mistakes [k] which could damage reputation [an] • Higher cost of training [k] which will increase expenses [an] • May be more willing to learn [k] so can quickly adapt to business requirements [an] • Skills can be taught but it is not easy to change someone’s personality [k] Experience: • May expect higher wages [k] increasing labour costs [an] • Likely to make less mistakes or provide better service [k] reducing amount of waste [an] • Reduces possible number of candidates [k] so could take more time to recruit [an] • Know they have ability/skills to do the job [an] Justification might include: Experience means a person already knows how to respond to customers in most situations straightaway [k] which can help the business offer a better/more professional service to its customers [an] which can encourage customer loyalty [an]. However, you can teach someone these skills more easily than you can change their personality. Personality is more important because you need people who are willing to learn/able to interact naturally with customers especially in a tertiary sector business where good customer service is important. [eval] [eval] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. 2

example_question: Do you think making a profit is more important for a business than managing its cash flow? Justify your answer.
model_answer: Do you think making a profit is more important for a business than 6 managing its cash flow? Justify your answer. Profit: • It is a source of finance may increase opportunity for growth [a n] • Reward for risk taking / allows dividend for owners without it, owners will have less incentive to invest • Reward for enterprise • Indicator/measure of success • Important for long term survival Cash flow: • Need cash to pay day to day costs / ensures liquidity/ help avoid cash flow problems/pay debt otherwise might have in sufficient funds / working capital to continue production • Sales may be on credit therefore money received later • Reduced need for overdraft which would add to interest costs Justification might include: Profit is a reward for risk taking , without it owners will have less incentive to invest . Cash flow is needed to pay day to day costs to ensure sufficient inventory to continue production. Managing cash flow is likely to be more important because a business can continue
notes: This is a general question so there are no marks for application. Allow attracts investors/investment as a [k] or [an] once Do not allow ‘ important for growth’ without the idea that it is a source of finance / or for reinvestment. Notes

example_question: Do you think selling to a wholesaler is the best channel of distribution for a start-up business to use? Justify your answer.
model_answer: Do you think selling to a wholesaler is the best channel of distribution for a start up business to use? Justify your answer. Wholesaler: • Likely to buy in large quantities increasing sales • Can hold less inventory less storage space/costs • Only deliver to one location lowering transport cost s • No direct contact/feedback with customer so difficult to build customer relationship/loyalty • Price may be higher leading to fewer sales • Lose (some) control over marketing/advertising • Wholesaler takes part of the profit which lowers profit margin 1(e) Other channels might include: • Sell direct (to customers)/ecommerce/online so has more control over its marketing/build rapport • Retailers so can reach wider range of customers • Agents as will have local knowledge so higher sales Justification might include: A wholesaler will help save storage space which reduces storage costs . They could sell online which would increase its access to customers . A wholesaler is the best channel to us
notes: 6 This is a general question so there are no marks for application. Points must be from viewpoint of the start up business. Some point can be written as [k] or [an] but only award once e.g. lower storage costs. Some points can be presented as advantages/ disadvantages of different channels but only award point once.
